sourceMovie.title: The Shining
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Shining for the same reason it broke through in 1980: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Stanley Kubrick keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Conjuring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Shining clicked for you, The Conjuring (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Shining clicked for you, Saw III (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Shining?
faq.answer: Try The Conjuring, Saw III, Insidious: Chapter 2—each shares DNA with The Shining while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Shining?
faq.answer: The Shining (1980) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Shining part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Shining Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
